---
title: "LaTeX for Logicians"
page-layout: article
format:
  html:
    include-before-body: _lfl-nav.html
---

These LaTeX for Logicians pages started life in 2004, before there was such a wealth of general material available online about LaTeX. Internet searches -- and AI assistants -- can now give all the information and help you could need. But perhaps there is still a small role for some pages assembling selected links to specific resources of particular interest to logicians using LaTeX. 

- *[Logic symbols](symbols.qmd)*. Lists of available logic and other symbols.

- *[Natural deduction proofs](nd.qmd)*. Packages for laying out natural deduction and sequent proofs in Gentzen style, and natural deduction proofs in Fitch style.

- *[Tree/tableau proofs](trees.qmd)*. Packages for downward-branching trees.

- *[Diagrams](diagrams.qmd)*. General programs for diagram construction. How to produce commutative diagrams for category theory in particular.

- *[In the classroom](classroom.qmd)*. This page gives links to resources which are especially useful for classroom use (e.g., packages for producing slides for data projectors, for constructing problem sheets, etc.).

- *[Logicians' miscellany](miscellany.qmd)*. Links to a variety of other resources which could be particularly useful for logicians.

- *[Fonts, etc.](fonts.qmd)* Links to additional information about fonts suitable for texts with a substantial amount of maths/symbols. Also a note on resources for long documents.

Thanks to all the contributors to LaTeX for Logicians over the years! I don't aim to be completist, but suggestions for relevant additions arealways welcome.
